seoTitle: South Grafton vs South Gundagai — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Grafton and South Gundagai. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Grafton vs South Gundagai: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Grafton and South Gundagai.
heading: South Grafton vs South Gundagai — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Grafton, New South Wales and South Gundagai, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Grafton has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to South Gundagai (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Grafton or South Gundagai safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Grafton has a flood risk score of 36/100, while South Gundagai scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Grafton scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. South Gundagai scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Grafton or South Gundagai?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Grafton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
